---
title: "Projects"
description: "Synthetic project and deliverable records for the v0.1 reference lifecycle."
listing:
  - id: project-catalog
    contents:
      - "da-eo-001/index.qmd"
    type: table
    fields: [title, project-id, description, iteration, phase, owner, steward]
  - id: deliverable-overview
    contents:
      - "da-eo-001/deliverables/*.qmd"
    type: table
    fields: [title, deliverable-id, project-id, status, owner, reviewer]
    sort: "deliverable-id"
---

Every record on this page is fictional and synthetic. It exists to demonstrate structure and lifecycle behavior without production participant, partner, project, location, or research data.

GitHub Issues would be authoritative for live operational and acceptance status. These Quarto listings render repository metadata for orientation; they do not update an Issue, persist feedback, or prove acceptance.

## Project catalog

::: {#project-catalog}
:::

## Deliverable overview

The text in the **status** column communicates lifecycle state without relying on color.

::: {#deliverable-overview}
:::

## Reading the examples

- Open the project title for context, ownership, decisions, limitations, and continuation guidance.
- Open a deliverable title for the artifact and review record.
- Use the [cohort handoff](da-eo-001/handoff.qmd) to see what a future team needs.

The static records deliberately contain no fabricated clickable GitHub Issues or private working-document links. In a live project, every deliverable would link to its real authoritative Issue and only access-safe working locations.
